statement: Every operation must have a unique operationId.
message: Operation must declare an operationId — the stable handle SDKs and agents call it by.
description: operationId is the stable, machine-usable name for an operation.
why: Without it, generated SDKs invent unstable names and agents cannot reference the operation.
what: Presence of operationId on each operation
when: On the pull request, before merge

statement: Every API must have a non-empty description.
message: The API must have a non-empty info.description.
description: A present, non-empty API description is the first thing a human or agent reads.
why: An undescribed API cannot be discovered or safely consumed by an agent.

statement: Schema property names must be camelCase.
message: Schema property names must be camelCase across the estate.
description: One property-casing convention makes payloads predictable across every API.
why: Mixed field casing forces per-endpoint mapping and invites bugs.

statement: GET operations must not define a request body.
message: GET operations must not declare a requestBody.
description: A GET with a body violates HTTP semantics and is stripped by many proxies.
why: Bodies on GET are unreliable across the HTTP ecosystem.
